title: The thin-oxide trap
intro: A thinner gate oxide grips the channel harder — better subthreshold slope, less drain mischief. So thin it to the limit, right? Careful: below ~0.8 nm of equivalent oxide, electrons quantum-tunnel straight through the gate. Find the oxide thickness that keeps BOTH leakage paths quiet.
explain: You rediscovered why high-k metal gate (HKMG) happened at the 45 nm node. Gate direct tunneling grows roughly 10× for every 0.25 nm of SiO₂-equivalent thinning — a hard exponential wall. High-k dielectrics restored a small EQUIVALENT thickness while keeping the physical barrier thick enough to block tunneling. In this simplified model you can only trade one leak against the other; real engineers changed the material instead.
